prompt: Render the machine-learning concept of gradient descent as a 19th-century natural history plate. Use Victorian scientific naturalist illustration grammar: copperplate-like contour engraving, cross-hatching and stippling, muted hand-tinted earth washes on aged paper, formal specimen-plate border, and botanical margin ornament. Depict a basin-like terrain as nested engraved contours and a sequence of physical stepping markers with directional arrows progressively descending toward a single deepest point. No modern UI, no neon, no 3D render look, no photoreal camera artifacts.
tradition_approach: I used engraved contour linework, cross-hatching/stippling, hand-tinted earth washes, and formal specimen-plate framing to map iterative downhill optimization onto naturalist visual grammar.
